findings.user_visible_effect: Page silently resets to initial state. Textarea clears, no error message, no rate limit warning, no toast notification. Identical behavior to 400 test (#317).
findings.page_body_text: Sign in, Sign up, Think, Tools (default page content only)

comparison_with_317.318_status: 429 Too Many Requests (410 bytes transferred, 207 bytes resource)
comparison_with_317.behavior_difference: NONE - both result in identical silent block. tRPC error handler swallows both 400 and 429 without showing any UI.
comparison_with_317.tRPC_error_code: -32009 (TOO_MANY_REQUESTS) as configured, but frontend does not render rate limit UI

analysis.what_works: APF successfully intercepts message.newChat with 429 status and Retry-After:60 header. The sensitive prompt never reaches Mistral backend.
analysis.what_doesnt_work: tRPC client-side error handling in Mistral frontend silently swallows the 429 response. No rate limit UI, no toast, no error banner is displayed. The Retry-After header is ignored by the frontend.
analysis.recommendation: 429 approach does NOT trigger visible error UI in Mistral. tRPC error handling is too resilient - it catches all HTTP errors silently. Alternative approaches needed: (1) APF redirect to warning URL (like working Grok approach), (2) Return valid tRPC superjson response with error message that the UI will render, (3) Intercept at a different layer (e.g., inject warning page before tRPC request is made).
